purpose: Refresh the parent regime taxonomy from web papers and GitHub sources after the user corrected that previous regimes were child classes.
latest_user_correction: These are child regimes; the main categories should include bull expansion, bear expansion, manipulation, consolidation, and possibly other larger classes.

mandatory_parent_roots.definition: Directional upside expansion or markup regime with positive drift/trend persistence and enough breadth/liquidity participation to permit long-risk continuation logic.
Directional downside expansion or markdown regime with negative drift/trend persistence, de-risking pressure, or persistent lower-low/lower-high structure.
Range, balance, compression, or accumulation/distribution regime where directional continuation is not the parent claim; allowed actions differ from expansion regimes.
High-volatility, jump, correlation, liquidity, or structural-break stress regime that is not safely collapsed into ordinary BearExpansion.

Market-integrity event class such as pump-and-dump, spoofing/layering, wash trading, self-trading, or other direct manipulation evidence. It is top-level for gating, but it still cannot be accepted from OHLCV-only proxies.

watchlist_parent_roots.definition: Accumulation/distribution, macro rotation, or pre-breakout/reversal transition. Promote only if it has source labels and a downstream action difference not already covered by ConsolidationBalance or CrisisDislocation.

compatibility_mapping.old_MainRegimeV2_Bull: candidate input to BullExpansion only after expansion/trend participation validation
compatibility_mapping.old_MainRegimeV2_Bear: candidate input to BearExpansion only after expansion/trend participation validation
compatibility_mapping.old_Manipulation_overlay: candidate input to ManipulationIntegrityEvent only with direct rows/windows and negative controls

source_refresh.use: Bull/bear cycle literature supports directional parent roots, but not enough by itself to distinguish expansion from non-expansion.
HMM posterior/state tooling is useful for persistence/probability features, but state ids are not independent source labels.
Change-point tooling supports boundary timing and crisis/dislocation evidence; it does not assign economic parent labels alone.
Jump-model tooling supports discontinuous regime/stress separation and the case for CrisisDislocation as a parent class.
Direct manipulation candidate with symbol, group, date, UTC hour, and exchange for pump-and-dump events. Needs a formal positive/negative-control gate before acceptance.
Crypto pump-and-dump literature supports ManipulationIntegrityEvent as a different evidence family from price-root regimes.
Regulator/SRO manipulation surfaces support market-integrity events as a separate audit class, but catalog/report pages are not automatically row-level labels.
Wash-trade positive-list candidate exposes NFT/token and tx_hash fields; current sampled CSV lacks timestamp and negative controls, so it is provenance until formally audited.

next_action: Materialize an ActionableRegimeRootV7 schema/crosswalk and then acquire/calibrate source labels separately for BullExpansion, BearExpansion, ConsolidationBalance, CrisisDislocation, and direct ManipulationIntegrityEvent.
